title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored 36 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-102 victory over the Atlanta Hawks.
Celtics played without Jaylen Brown due to Achilles tendinitis; Jayson Tatum added 25 points and 12 rebounds.
Jordan Walsh contributed defensively with three blocks, earning significant minutes in Brown's absence.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics this season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles’ tendon.
Tatum was motivated to return by the Celtics' strong performance and their contention for a championship.
Since returning, Tatum averages 9.2 rebounds per game, though his shooting percentages remain below career averages.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment on Pawtucket Boulevard Friday afternoon, per Middlesex DA's Office.
The shooting followed an altercation between two armed men who knew each other from the building, officials reported.
Police confirmed no ongoing public threat; the incident is under investigation by local and state authorities.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 after being purchased by the Rockets' owners, the Fertitta family.
The Sun's franchise will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the former team disbanded in 2008.
The sale, reportedly at $300 million, marks a WNBA record amid a new collective bargaining agreement.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' starring Ana de Armas is now streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice' featuring Vince Vaughn is available for streaming on Hulu.
'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 with Charlie Cox is streaming on Disney+.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown missing Celtics game against Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis as reported on Friday.
Head coach Joe Mazzulla states Brown's condition is day-to-day after treatment before Friday's matchup.
Celtics currently stand second in Eastern Conference with a 48-24 record despite injuries to key players.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington have implemented bans on gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
73% of over 250 Boston.com poll respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality and minimal environmental impact.
Supporters of the ban highlight issues like noise and fumes, arguing that gas blowers disrupt neighborhoods.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 COVID-19 variant, also known as the 'cicada' variant, has been detected in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2, first found in South Africa in November 2024, has been detected in 132 U.S. wastewater samples.
Current COVID vaccines are expected to continue protecting against severe disease despite BA.3.2's ability to evade some antibodies.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that a lawsuit against two DCF employees for allegedly unlawfully seizing children in Waltham can proceed.
The children were removed from their Waltham home in 2022 despite no evidence of abuse, causing a four-month separation from their parents.
The case involves allegations of constitutional rights violations, with claims the children were seized without a warrant or parental consent.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food & Wine lists three New England restaurants among the 12 most anticipated U.S. openings this spring.
Willie's on Beacon Hill, from Best New Chef 2011 Jamie Bissonnette, offers globally influenced pizzas, pastas, and appetizers.
Ladyfish and La Tavernetta, opening in Portland and East Boston, feature unique seafood and coastal tavern concepts.
